articles.title: Delhi EV policy keeps hybrids out, includes 3-year lock-in period
articles.description: Delhi's new EV policy, effective July 1, prioritizes pure electric vehicles, excluding hybrids. It offers substantial road tax and registration fee exemptions for electric cars under Rs 30 lakh, alongside a Rs 1 lakh incentive for scrapping old cars to buy new EVs. Subsidies are also available for electric two and three-wheelers, aiming to significantly curb pollution and boost clean mobility.

articles.title: Bengal slashes 65 Muslim sub-groups from TMC-era list, clears twin OBC quota bills
articles.description: West Bengal's assembly has passed two bills to revise the OBC reservation framework, now recognizing 66 communities (54 Hindu, 12 Muslim) outside SC/ST categories. This move reverses changes made by the previous TMC government, which had faced legal challenges. The new legislation restores greater power to the West Bengal Commission for Backward Classes, making its recommendations binding on the government for determining reservation percentages and community classifications.
articles.full_text: West Bengal's assembly has passed two bills to revise the OBC reservation framework, now recognizing 66 communities (54 Hindu, 12 Muslim) outside SC/ST categories. The new legislation restores greater power to the West Bengal Commission for Backward Classes, making its recommendations binding on the government for determining reservation percentages and community classifications.

articles.title: Saibari’s winner sends Morocco into last 16 as Netherlands crash to earliest WC exit
articles.description: Morocco stunned the Netherlands in a dramatic penalty shootout, securing a 3-2 victory after a 1-1 draw to advance to the last 16 of the FIFA World Cup. Goalkeeper Yassine Bounou's crucial save paved the way for Ismael Saibari's winning penalty. The Atlas Lions fought back from Cody Gakpo's late goal, forcing extra time with a stoppage-time header, ending the Dutch's World Cup campaign.

articles.title: Candace Owens' Erika Kirk criticism takes surprising turn after controversial comments about her husband George Farmer
articles.description: Candace Owens is facing fresh online scrutiny after comments about her husband, George Farmer, overshadowed her latest criticism of Erika Kirk. While questioning reports about Charlie and Erika Kirk's close communication, Owens said, “I don’t know what George does every day. I’m in my basement all day.” The remark sparked debate online, with many arguing it revealed more about her own marriage than her ongoing criticism of Erika Kirk.

articles.title: Who is TS Baby Jayne? Influencer shares unseen photos, alleges past relationship with Chudthebuilder
articles.description: TS Baby Jayne, a transgender tattoo artist and content creator, has gone viral after claiming she dated streamer Chudthebuilder in 2025. In a YouTube video, she shared photos and alleged messages to support her account. The evidence has not been independently verified, and Chudthebuilder has not publicly responded, leaving the claims the subject of intense online debate.

articles.title: India’s economy could hit $7 trillion this decade, double by mid-2030s
articles.description: India's economy is projected to reach $7 trillion by 2030, with ambitions for $14 trillion by mid-2030s and $25-30 trillion by 2047, according to Ambassador Vinay Mohan Kwatra. Meanwhile, the US Ambassador to India, Sergio Gor, expressed optimism about finalizing an interim trade deal soon and hinted at a potential visit by President Trump to India next year, underscoring strengthening bilateral ties.

articles.title: 'We're in the final steps': US ambassador Sergio Gor signals progress on India-US trade deal
articles.description: US Ambassador Sergio Gor announced that the US-India trade agreement is in its final stages, with only a small percentage of issues remaining. He expressed confidence in a swift conclusion after 18 months of talks, highlighting the pact's potential to bring greater certainty and stability for businesses. Bilateral trade has surged significantly, with both nations aiming for a $500 billion target.

articles.title: Gold, Silver Rate Today Live Updates: Gold prices tank over 1%, set for fourth consecutive monthly decline
articles.full_text: Although Iranian and US negotiators were expected to meet in Doha this week, Iran said on Monday that no talks had been scheduled after fresh missile exchanges over the weekend put the interim ceasefire, which had ended the four-month conflict, under renewed strain. In the United States, the Supreme Court on Monday declined to allow President Donald Trump to dismiss Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook, reaffirming the central bank's independence in the face of an unprecedented challenge from the Republican president. According to the CME FedWatch Tool, traders are pricing in three Federal Reserve interest rate hikes this year, with around a 63% probability of a September increase. Investors are now awaiting the release of June's ADP employment report and the US nonfarm payrolls data later this week for additional clues on the Federal Reserve's future monetary policy. Guinea is seeking to establish itself as a regional hub for gold refining, its mines minister said, as part of a broader effort by West African producers to process bullion domestically instead of exporting it to the Middle East and other regions. Among other precious metals, spot silver fell 2.4% to $56.89 per ounce. Platinum declined 1.1% to $1,557.08 per ounce, while palladium slipped 0.5% to $1,207 per ounce. All three metals were also on track to end the month in negative territory. Gold prices fell by more than 1% on Tuesday and remained on course for a fourth straight monthly loss as investor focus shifted from geopolitical tensions in the Middle East to the likelihood of further interest rate increases by the US Federal Reserve to curb inflation. Spot gold declined 1.5% to $3,957.74 per ounce as of 0112 GMT and was on track to register a monthly decline of 12.7%. US gold futures for August delivery dropped 1.7% to $3,971.60 per ounce. The US dollar was set to end the month higher, making gold more expensive for buyers using other currencies. Indian households are estimated to own nearly 30,000 tonnes of gold, making the country one of the world's largest holders of the precious metal. Yet much of it sits idle in lockers and cupboards. As gold prices remain historically high, organised players see a major opportunity to bring these reserves back into the economy through recycling. Augmont, which has expanded its Gold For All network to 114 centres, says more consumers are exploring ways to assess, recycle and monetise their holdings. Industry experts believe this shift could reduce import dependence while unlocking enormous economic value. Companies that purchase old gold are witnessing a sharp jump in customer footfall. Muthoot Exim said gold collections across its network of more than 100 Gold Points have surged by 40%. CEO Keyur Shah said consumers are increasingly choosing organised and transparent platforms to convert unused jewellery into cash. The company refines purchased gold into 24-carat purity before supplying it to jewellery and gold coin manufacturers. The process not only unlocks value for consumers but also brings dormant gold back into circulation, strengthening India's domestic gold ecosystem. The surge in gold sales is breathing new life into India's organised gold recycling sector. High prices are encouraging households to bring idle jewellery into the formal market, providing refiners and jewellers with a steady supply of recyclable gold. India imported gold worth nearly $72.4 billion in FY26, but recycled gold already contributes an estimated 125-150 tonnes annually. Industry estimates suggest this could rise to 200-250 tonnes in 2026 if current trends continue, helping reduce dependence on expensive imports while improving domestic availability of the precious metal. Gold and silver are expected to stay under pressure in the coming week as investors weigh renewed US-Iran tensions, fluctuations in crude oil prices, and a string of key macroeconomic data releases, analysts said. Market participants will closely track manufacturing and services PMI readings across major economies, Eurozone inflation data, as well as US nonfarm payrolls and unemployment figures for fresh cues on the Federal Reserve’s monetary policy direction. Geopolitical developments are also likely to remain in focus after US-Iran negotiations stalled following a sharp escalation in military conflict, they added. Gold price today: Why are Indians selling the yellow metal Silver prices came under pressure in the domestic market as global uncertainties continued to weigh on sentiment, said Gaurav Garg, Research Analyst at Lemonn Markets Desk. In international trade, spot gold slipped by $54.14, or 1.32%, to $4,035.12 per ounce, while silver dropped nearly 3% to USD 57.62 per ounce. “Crude oil prices and the US dollar index edged higher amid renewed escalation in US-Iran tensions over the weekend,” said Praveen Singh, Head of Commodities & Currencies at Mirae Asset Sharekhan. However, Washington and Tehran later agreed to pause hostilities after fresh military developments threatened a fragile 60-day understanding, though investor caution continued to keep bullion under pressure. "Crude oil prices in the global markets and US dollar index have edged higher on re-escalation of the US-Iran tensions during the weekend", said Saumil Gandhi, Senior Analyst – Commodities at HDFC Securities. Gold prices recovered in the national capital, ending a three-session losing streak as value buying by traders pushed the precious metal higher. According to the All India Sarafa Association, gold of 99.9% purity rose by Rs 1,300 to Rs 1,46,600 per 10 grams (inclusive of all taxes), up from its previous close of Rs 1,45,300 per 10 grams on Thursday. Bullion markets remained shut on Friday on account of Muharram. Silver, however, continued its downward trend for a fourth straight session. The white metal declined by Rs 2,000 to Rs 2,24,000 per kilogram (inclusive of all taxes), compared with Rs 2,26,000 per kg in the previous session. Silver prices declined by ₹746 to ₹2,22,726 per kilogram on Monday as traders pared their positions in the commodity. On the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX), September silver futures slipped ₹746, or 0.33%, to settle at ₹2,22,726 per kg, with a turnover of 1,988 lots. According to analysts, the decline was primarily driven by fresh selling from market participants, which weighed on silver prices. In the international market, however, spot silver was trading 1.04% higher at USD 58.53 per ounce in New York. Today, oil prices and US Dollar Index have edged higher on re-escalation of the US-Iran tensions in the weekend. Consequently , gold is trading slightly lower. US and Iran having agreed to halt attacks will meet in Qatar on Tuesday. Iran insists on controlling the Strait of Hormuz. Gold extended its weekly losing streak to the fourth straight week in the week ending June 26, though, supported by a slightly weaker US Dollar and softer yields, It closed with a daily gain of 1.53% at $4089 on June 26. This week is crucial for the metal as US nonfarm payroll report for June will be released on July 2 Renewed Middle East tensions pose a risk to the ceasefire and peace talks, which will keep oil prices supported; thus, limiting upside in gold. Gold, currently trading 0.60% lower at $4064, is expected to consolidate with a bearish tilt. Support is at $4000/$3960. Resistance is at $4100/$4170, says Praveen Singh, Head of commodities at Mirae Asset ShareKhan. Gold and silver traded largely flat on the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX) on Monday, even as fresh tensions in the Middle East heightened concerns over the stability of the Iran-US ceasefire. At the same time, expectations that the US Federal Reserve could continue raising interest rates kept pressure on the non-yielding precious metals. MCX gold futures for August delivery declined by ₹617, or 0.4%, to ₹1,43,545 per 10 grams. Contracts scheduled for October and December expiry also traded lower, slipping by as much as 0.4%. Silver futures for September delivery fell ₹518 to ₹2,22,954 per kilogram. Contracts with July and December expiries also weakened, registering losses of up to 0.3%. "US and Iran were at it again over the weekend, with fresh military strikes reported from both parties, which casts further doubt over how long oil can stay at these subdued levels and therefore over the broader inflation and interest rate outlook," said Tim Waterer, Chief Market Analyst at KCM Trade. Crude oil prices advanced after Iran launched missiles and drones targeting US military facilities in Kuwait and Bahrain early on Sunday. The attacks came shortly after US President Donald Trump warned that the Iranian leadership would face severe consequences if it failed to honour the agreement aimed at ending the conflict. Despite the escalation, Tehran and Washington later agreed to suspend the latest hostilities in the Gulf and resume negotiations over their dispute concerning the Strait of Hormuz, according to an Axios report published on Sunday. Higher crude prices can add to inflationary pressures and increase the likelihood of further interest rate hikes. Although gold is widely regarded as a hedge against inflation, its attractiveness tends to diminish when interest rates rise because it does not generate any yield. “Gold prices traded lower as investors remained cautious amid renewed geopolitical tensions and expectations that the US Federal Reserve could maintain a restrictive monetary policy for longer. Safe-haven demand received limited support after fresh exchanges between the US and Iran over the weekend strained the fragile ceasefire, while a stronger US dollar and elevated Treasury yields continue to weigh on bullion. The recent US-Iran peace framework had eased concerns over energy-driven inflation by pushing oil prices back toward pre-war levels, but renewed attacks on vessels near the Strait of Hormuz have revived uncertainty over regional stability. Meanwhile, the US core PCE price index, the Fed’s preferred inflation gauge, remained in line with expectations but climbed to its highest annual level since October 2023, reinforcing the view that inflation remains persistent. Markets continue to price in the possibility of another Fed rate hike by the end of 2026, Investors will now closely monitor this week’s US consumer confidence, ADP employment, jobless claims and nonfarm payrolls data for further clarity on the Federal Reserve’s policy outlook and the direction of the US dollar, which are likely to remain the key drivers for gold prices,” says Manav Modi Commodities Analyst Motilal Oswal Financial services Ltd. Gold prices moved lower on Monday as fresh military action between the United States and Iran drove crude oil prices higher, while growing expectations of further interest rate hikes by the US Federal Reserve continued to weigh on the non-yielding precious metal. Spot gold fell 0.7% to $4,061.35 per ounce as of 0242 GMT. US gold futures for August delivery were down 0.5% at $4,076.40 per ounce. The metal was on course to register its fourth straight monthly decline, with losses of about 10.4%. Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Sunday expressed his appreciation to the people of India for responding positively to the appeals he had made in the wake of the ongoing crisis in West Asia, saying citizens had gone beyond merely endorsing his requests and were actively following them. Addressing the nation during the 135th edition of his monthly radio programme, Mann Ki Baat, the Prime Minister said, "In view of the prevalent war-like situation in West Asia, I had urged countrymen on a few points. I had asked people to avoid buying gold for some time, as far as possible. I am grateful to every citizen of the country; not only have they supported my appeal, they are also actively cooperating in every way." "Gold remained under significant selling pressure last week, ending lower by 2 per cent, extending its week-on-week decline as persistent US dollar strength continued to weigh on precious metals," said Jateen Trivedi, Vice President – Research Analyst, Commodity and Currency, LKP Securities. He said crude oil prices fell nearly 10% during the week, easing inflation concerns and reducing gold's appeal as an inflation hedge. Investors also continued to favour the US dollar over bullion. Indian households are increasingly cashing in on old jewellery as concerns grow that gold prices may have already reached their peak. According to data from the India Bullion & Jewellers Association (IBJA), consumers sold nearly 50 tonnes of old gold during the April-June quarter, marking a 43% increase compared with the same period last year. After gold prices climbed to around ₹1.4 lakh per 10 grams and expectations emerged that they could fall further to ₹1.2 lakh, many consumers have preferred selling old jewellery to realise profits instead of exchanging it for new ornaments. "Indian consumers are leveraging the high price of gold for liquid cash," said Surendra Mehta, National Secretary of IBJA to ET. "Though prices have fallen from a high of ₹1.8 lakh per 10 gm in the beginning of the year to ₹1.4 lakh, there is a fear among them that prices may further crash to ₹1.2 lakh, which is prompting them to sell gold to encash." The trend has also provided a boost to the organised gold recycling industry, with elevated prices encouraging households to bring dormant gold holdings back into the market rather than keeping them idle. Industry executives said consumers are increasingly viewing gold as a financial asset that can be monetised when prices are favourable, resulting in a steady flow of recyclable gold for refiners and jewellers. Companies engaged in buying old gold are witnessing a similar rise in business. Muthoot Exim reported a 40% jump in old gold collections across its network of more than 100 Gold Points located across the country. "For gold and silver prices, the momentum still remains down and corrective," said Pranav Mer, Vice President, EBG – Commodity & Currency Research, JM Financial Services Ltd Mer noted that gold recovered modestly on Friday as bargain buying emerged after US Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) data showed inflation had risen at a slower pace than in the previous month. He added that continued gold purchases by China's central bank following the latest US-Iran strikes, along with President Donald Trump's threat to impose 100% tariffs on the European Union, also lent support to prices. However, gains remained limited due to higher US Treasury yields, while silver continued to face pressure from weakness in industrial metals, a stronger US dollar and subdued demand, he said. The steep decline in gold prices over the past five months has led to margin calls on certain gold loans, particularly those structured as bullet repayment loans, while loans involving regular monthly repayments have largely remained unaffected, according to people familiar with the matter. Domestic gold prices have fallen nearly 22% from the record highs recorded in the final week of January. The precious metal had already corrected by about 15% in March amid the West Asia conflict before moving within a narrow range for some time. Selling pressure intensified again after the US Federal Reserve indicated that interest rates could remain elevated for a longer period. The price of 24-carat gold in India currently stands at around ₹1.40 lakh per 10 grams, down from its peak of ₹1.82 lakh reached on January 29. A margin call occurs when lenders require borrowers to either repay a portion of their outstanding loan or provide additional collateral. In the case of gold loans, a decline in gold prices reduces the value of the pledged jewellery or bullion, increasing the loan-to-value (LTV) ratio if the loan amount remains unchanged, thereby prompting lenders to seek additional margin. The impact has been more pronounced in bullet repayment loans, where borrowers repay both the principal and the accumulated interest in a single payment at the end of the loan tenure instead of making regular monthly instalments, according to an ET report. Since the principal outstanding remains unchanged throughout the loan period, these loans are more exposed to a decline in the value of the underlying collateral.

articles.description: "Tickets worth Rupees 52,36,000 were unsold. Why all the tickets were not sold-out? DDCA started online ticket sale quite late that too without proper advertisement," Khanna claimed in a media release.

articles.title: RRB Technician Notification 2026-27 released with 6557 vacancies under CEN 02/2026; apply here
articles.description: The Railway Recruitment Board has released the RRB Technician Notification 2026-27 under CEN 02/2026, announcing 6,557 vacancies for Technician Grade I Signal and Technician Grade III posts. The online application process begins on June 30, 2026, and ends on July 29, 2026. Eligible candidates with Matriculation and ITI qualifications can apply. The selection process includes CBT, document verification and medical examination. Vacancy distribution and pay levels have also been detailed in the official notification.
articles.full_text: The Railway Recruitment Board has released the RRB Technician Notification 2026-27 under CEN 02/2026, announcing 6,557 vacancies for Technician Grade I Signal and Technician Grade III posts. The online application process begins on June 30, 2026, and ends on July 29, 2026. Eligible candidates with Matriculation and ITI qualifications can apply. The selection process includes CBT, document verification and medical examination. Vacancy distribution and pay levels have also been detailed in the official notification.

articles.title: Noida schools revise Classes 1 to 8 timings as heat advisory issued in Gautam Buddh Nagar
articles.description: The Gautam Buddh Nagar administration has revised school timings for Classes 1 to 8 in Noida and Greater Noida due to prevailing heat conditions. From June 29, 2026, schools will function from 7:00 AM to 12:00 PM until further orders. The directive applies to government, aided, recognised and all board-affiliated schools. The decision follows continued high temperatures across north India as the delayed southwest monsoon keeps hot and humid conditions in the region.
articles.full_text: The Gautam Buddh Nagar administration has revised school timings for Classes 1 to 8 in Noida and Greater Noida due to prevailing heat conditions. From June 29, 2026, schools will function from 7:00 AM to 12:00 PM until further orders. The directive applies to government, aided, recognised and all board-affiliated schools. The decision follows continued high temperatures across north India as the delayed southwest monsoon keeps hot and humid conditions in the region.

articles.title: Class 10 student Mahima Rajput selected for Mission ShakthiSAT with participants from 108 countries
articles.description: Fourteen-year-old Class 10 student Mahima Rajput has been selected from India for Mission ShakthiSAT, an international student space programme involving participants from 108 countries. After completing a training curriculum comprising 21 modules and 365 lessons, she will travel to Delhi for satellite integration activities beginning August 23. The mission, led by Space Kidz India, is scheduled for launch in October and aims to provide students with practical experience in satellite development and space science.

articles.title: Arijit Singh goes viral for dancing to ‘Chaleya’
articles.description: Arijit Singh has taken his fans by surprise with his unexpected dance performance of 'Chaleya' from the blockbuster 'Pathaan'. A captivating video of him showcasing his dance skills has since gone viral, garnering admiration from many who initially thought it was AI-generated. This thrilling display comes after Singh's recent decision to step back from Bollywood playback singing to pursue independent music ventures.

articles.title: 'Con City' adds Rs 48 lakh on Day 4
articles.description: Tamil crime comedy 'Con City' is drawing praise for its engaging narrative and strong performances from Arjun Das and Anna Ben. Despite a slow Monday, the film garnered positive word-of-mouth, contributing to a decent opening weekend. While some viewers laud its clever plot and casting, others point to minor logical inconsistencies and character development issues. The movie continues to entertain audiences with its unique blend of humor and crime.

articles.title: Who is Thylane Blondeau? Former 'most beautiful girl'
articles.description: Thylane Blondeau, recognized as the 'most beautiful girl in the world,' has tied the knot with her long-term partner, actor and DJ Ben Attal, in a charming town hall wedding in Paris. This romantic event came just three months after they got engaged in Greece. Dazzling in a custom couture gown, Blondeau’s wedding signifies a beautiful union between two influential figures in the French entertainment and fashion industries.

articles.title: 'House of the Dragon' COPIED 'Magnificent Century: Kosem'? - WATCH
articles.description: Emma D'Arcy's portrayal of grief in 'House of the Dragon' is drawing significant praise, with the latest episode achieving a record rating. However, a viral comparison has emerged, highlighting striking visual and emotional parallels to a scene from the Turkish drama 'Magnificent Century: Kosem'.
